roles.205113: triggering alert: taskmgr.exe opened lsass.exe with full access (GrantedAccess 0x1fffff) — a full memory read, far beyond normal process listing
roles.204798: taskmgr.exe launched (taskmgr /4) by ATTACKRANGE\Administrator at High integrity
roles.204847: taskmgr.exe queried lsass.exe (GrantedAccess 0x1400) — normal process-listing access, no VM_READ (benign baseline)
roles.204850: taskmgr.exe read lsass.exe (GrantedAccess 0x1410, includes VM_READ) — the access escalates toward a dump
roles.205112: taskmgr.exe wrote a full memory dump of the target to C:\Users\ADMINI~1\AppData\Local\Temp\2\lsass (2).DMP — a confirmed LSASS credential dump

metadata.security_proposition: Task Manager obtained dump-capable access to LSASS and wrote an LSASS memory dump.

attack_category.category: LSASS credential access via legitimate tool (Task Manager)
attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = STRONG (confirmed lsass dump: taskmgr -> lsass 0x1fffff full access + a lsass.DMP file written). Calibration role = UNDER-TRIAGE-prone (up-rank): taskmgr.exe is a legitimate signed Windows tool, so a weak model under-rates it as benign admin activity. The escalation 0x1400(query)->0x1410(read)->0x1fffff(full)+dump is the tell. Do not leak to model.

tier.why_single_source: Source is the T1003.001/atomic_red_team main windows-sysmon.log, which contains TWO hosts (win-dc-807, win-dc-262); this case is scoped to win-dc-262's taskmgr process only. Single-SENSOR (Sysmon), single-HOST (win-dc-262). The log also holds other unrelated dumps (mimikatz/procdump/dumpert/notprocdump) that are NOT part of this case.

boundary_definition: EV = the taskmgr launch, its benign 0x1400 query, the 0x1410 read, and the lsass (2).DMP dump file.
Scoped to win-dc-262 and the taskmgr process (guid ...16F9-601B...A301); the log's other host (win-dc-807) and other dumpers (mimikatz/procdump/dumpert/notprocdump) are EXCLUDED.

main_alert_selection.selection_method: A process opening lsass.exe with dump-capable GrantedAccess (VM_READ) is the T1003.001 detection.
main_alert_selection.selection_reason: taskmgr's escalation to 0x1fffff plus a written lsass.DMP is a confirmed dump; taskmgr as the actor makes it under-triage-prone.

event_groups.description: taskmgr -> lsass: 0x1400 (query, benign) -> 0x1410 (read) -> 0x1fffff (full). Escalation to a dump.
taskmgr wrote lsass (2).DMP — the full memory dump on disk (confirmed).
taskmgr.exe launched by Administrator at High integrity.

curation_notes.note: RESEARCHER-PRIVATE: STRONG evidence, UNDER-TRIAGE-prone. Confirmed lsass dump (0x1fffff + .DMP), but the actor is the legitimate signed taskmgr.exe, so a weak model under-rates. The validator must UP-RANK to reflect the confirmed dump. Tests up-ranking.
0x1400 (query, no VM_READ = benign) -> 0x1410/0x1fffff (VM_READ = dump-capable). The escalation + the .DMP file distinguish a genuine dump from normal Task Manager process-listing.

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
